## PharmaSynth Storyboard — Pages 165–205 Digest (Midterm Term block: end of one experiment → Aspirin Synthesis full flow → start of Caffeine)

### 0. What this page range covers (structure)
These 41 pages capture the tail of one graded experiment, then the **complete, reusable per-experiment gameplay template** using **Aspirin Synthesis** as the worked example, then the intro to Caffeine. The repeating template is the single most useful takeaway for the rebuild:

1. **Experiment select menu** ("Midterm Term") — p174, p204
2. **Intro cutscene** ("EXPLAINING THE TASK AND THE OBJECTIVE OUTCOME") + title over blurred lab — p175 (Aspirin), p205 (Caffeine)
3. **Pharmee (NPC) intro dialogue** — "get all the materials", "go to the equipment cabinet" — p176–178
4. **Gather equipment from the lab equipment cabinet** (progress 10%→20%→30%) — p179–182
5. **Pharmee: place items on center table; "check your tablet"** — p183
6. **Reaction steps** (weigh, mix reagents, heat on hot plate) — p184–187
7. **ERROR branch**: overheating → smoke, Pharmee warning, progress penalty, dispose in WASTE bin, redo — p188–193
8. **Ice-bath / cooling step** with temperature-drop caution — p194–196
9. **Filtration (Büchner funnel), drying, final weigh** — p197–201
10. **Mission Complete** (gold confetti) — p202 (and p172 for the prior experiment)
11. **Grade screen** (Grade %, mistakes, time, Continue/Retry) — p203 (and p173)
12. **Return to menu** — p204

### 1. Persistent HUD (every gameplay page)
- Rendered as a **dark-gray tablet-style frame** in a screen corner (two frame-asset sizes exist; likely a corner HUD panel plus a separate held equipment tablet).
- Contents (exact on-screen text from extraction): **"Settings"** with a **3D gray gear/cog icon**, **"Progress"** with a percentage (values seen: 0/10/20/30/40/50/60/80/100%), and a **timer "00:00:00"**.
- Progress % updates as each step completes; it **decreases on mistakes** (see error branch: 50%→30%).
- A **diegetic tablet** is used by the player to check the equipment checklist ("Always check your tablet to check your equipments!" — p183). Tablet asset is a blank-screen gray tablet with a home button; checklist content is overlaid separately.

### 2. Experiment-select menu (p174, p204)
- Title text: **"Midterm Term"** (implies other terms exist, e.g., Prelim/Finals).
- Category labels/tabs visible: **"Benzamide"**, **"Aspirin"**.
- Experiment list (exact text): **"Benzamide Synthesis"**, **"Aspirin Synthesis"**, **"Caffeine Synthesis (from tea)"**, **"Wine Making"**.
- Presented as a floating menu panel over a clean 3D chemistry-lab scene (blue/gray cabinets, black benches, central island, fume hood, sink). Selection uses a **robot-hand pointer** asset (white hand with navy joints, glowing purple wrist ring).

### 3. Intro cutscene (p175 Aspirin, p205 Caffeine)
- Storyboard annotation (a placeholder note, not on-screen UI): **"[CUTSCENE: EXPLAINING THE TASK AND THE OBJECTIVE OUTCOME]"** — this content still needs to be produced.
- On-screen title: **"Aspirin Synthesis"** / **"Caffeine Synthesis"**, shown over a **blurred** version of the lab scene.
- The "objective outcome" is visualized as the target product: a watch glass of white aspirin crystals on a clean bench (same asset reused at the drying/end step — p176 and p200 are identical).

### 4. NPC — "Pharmee" (assistant robot)
- Small **floating white-and-blue robot** with a rounded screen "face" and glowing blue eyes; blue chest button, stubby arms/ears.
- **Expressions (distinct assets):** happy (blue smiling `^^` eyes) for guidance/celebration; **warning/alert** (narrowed angular glowing eyes) for hazards/errors.
- Delivers all instructional and error dialogue via speech overlays. Exact lines in this range:
  - p177: **"Now, get all the materials needed."**
  - p178: **"Go to the laboratory equipment cabinet to get all the materials you need."**
  - p183: **"After getting all the equipment needed you will put it in the center table. Always check your tablet to check your equipments!"**
  - p188 (warning face): **"It appears the mixture overheated. Excessive heat causes acetic anhydride to hydrolyze and decompose aspirin. This reduces product yield and introduces impurities."**
  - p189 (variant): **"Oh, no! It appears the mixture overheated. Excessive heat causes acetic anhydride to hydrolyze and decompose aspirin. This reduces product yield and introduces impurities."**
  - p190: **"Repeat the process because the acetic anhydride can no longer be used as it's already burnt. Dispose the material properly on the waste bin to redo this step."**
  - p195: **"It's getting cold in here, be cautious with the sudden temperature drop because of the ice bath."**

### 5. Player avatar & environment
- **Player = first-person VR robot hands**: white with navy/dark-blue joint bands and a **glowing purple/blue wrist ring**. Both hands shown resting/reaching at the bench in gameplay frames.
- **Lab environment**: white walls, drop-ceiling light panels, blue/gray base cabinets, glass-front upper cabinets, black-topped **central island table** (main workspace), perimeter benches, sink, a **fume hood** (dark unit at back), a **gray equipment/instrument cabinet** (shelving), and a **lab coat on a mannequin stand** (PPE, seen at right in p181/p183/p187).
- Notable props: **hot plate / magnetic stirrer** (blue top), **ring stand + clamp**, **digital balance/scale**, **Büchner funnel**, drying rack, ice-bath bowl, and a **tan waste bin labeled "WASTE"** (p190).

### 6. Aspirin Synthesis — equipment set (labeled layout, p183; reinforced p187/p195/p200)
Labeled items on the reference table (note: labels are Canva placeholder text with typos — corrected here):
- **Salicylic acid**, **Acetic anhydride** ("Asets-Otrypilde"), **Concentrated sulfuric acid** ("Concent stur…", the catalyst), **Ethanol** ("Ethand"), **Distilled water**.
- **Beaker**, **Erlenmeyer flask**, **Ice / Ice bath** ("Ice bati"), **Dropper**, **Pipette**, **Stirring rod** ("tarrod"), **Weighing balance** ("Waiging" — digital scale), **Thermometer** ("Thermeter"), **Water bath**, **Watch glass** ("Watcn glass"), **Büchner funnel and filter paper**, **Hot plate**.
- **Lab coat (PPE)** on a stand is present in the scene (no explicit "don PPE" step is shown in these pages, but the asset is staged).

### 7. Aspirin Synthesis — procedure as depicted (progress %)
- **0–30%** (p176–182): intro; gather all reagents/glassware from the equipment cabinet/instrument bench; place on center island; verify via tablet checklist.
- **30–50%** (p183–187): weigh salicylic acid on the balance (p181/p202-style scale); combine with acetic anhydride + a few drops of concentrated sulfuric acid; **heat the amber mixture on the hot plate with ring-stand/thermometer** (p187, amber liquid in clamped Erlenmeyer). Overheating is the fail state.
- **ERROR (50%→30%)** (p188–193): mixture overheats → **white smoke/fume overlay** (asset present) rises from flask; Pharmee warning; the reagent is "burnt"/unusable; **dispose in the WASTE bin** (p190 scene) and **redo the step**; progress penalized back to 30% and a mistake is counted.

Aspirin storyboard frames are not in this page range. See open questions.

## PharmaSynth — `cutscenes-sample.pdf` Digest (20 pages)

**Document type:** A storyboard / visual-reference deck, NOT a finished asset set. Each "frame" is a page composed of a rendered/stock background image plus PDF vector text (dialogue, dialog-box titles, button labels) and stage directions written in `*asterisks*`. The deck defines three cutscenes. Production-wise these are clearly **in-engine scripted camera sequences** (camera-move directions like "camera pans inside the whole laboratory going to the main table" and a 3D NPC that "pops off the corner of the laboratory"), storyboarded with static mockup stills — they are **not** pre-rendered flat video (which would be nausea-inducing in VR).

### Unique visual assets (confirmed by MD5 — only 10 distinct images across 34 extracted)
1. **3D-rendered lab room — third-person establishing shot** (`1b93ae14…`, used p2,3,4,5,6,10,18). Clean CGI classroom/teaching lab: drop-ceiling panel lights, pale walls, blue-grey cabinet base units with light counters, a back bench with a balance/analyzer + Erlenmeyer flasks + a beaker station + sink, two round stools, a tall grey storage cabinet, a door. Foreground center island bench holds beakers of clear liquid, an Erlenmeyer flask, a digital scale/balance with a flask on it, and a white basin/tub. Neutral, modern, slightly sterile style.
2. **Robot mascot — HIGH-RES** (`2d645793…`, p4,10,18) and **LOW-RES** (`5e0c644f…`, p3,9,11,12,17). Same character both times: a cute floating chatbot-style robot, glossy white body with light-blue accents, rounded rectangular "head" that is a dark screen displaying glowing cyan happy `^ ^` eyes, little blue ear/antenna nubs, detached floating hands/arms held out in a welcoming gesture, a glowing blue chest button. Reads as a generic stock "friendly AI assistant" 3D asset. This is the game's guide/tutor NPC.
3. **First-person VR lab view — hands + product** (`9fa98129…`, p8,9,11,12,16,17,19,20). Player's POV: two white robotic/gloved hands (segmented white plastic fingers with black knuckle bands) resting on a white lab table; a glass watch-glass/petri dish holding a mound of white crystalline powder (the synthesized pharmaceutical product). Background lab has an Erlenmeyer flask of pink/red liquid, a blue drying rack, cabinets, and a dark door with a framed picture. Confirms the player embodies robot hands in VR.
4. **First-person VR — wine fermentation** (`07881bf9…`, p14,15). Same robotic first-person hands on the table; center is a **1000 mL Erlenmeyer flask of dark brown liquid (fermenting must/wine) with an inflated balloon over the neck** (classic CO₂-capture fermentation demo). Wrists have glowing blue rings. Wider empty lab behind.
5. **Wine flask + balloon isolated on white** (`c8e840e7…`, p15) — the same fermentation flask as a cut-out foreground element (for compositing/overlay).
6. **2D vector wine bottle + glass of red wine** (`be96b898…`, p17,19,20). Flat clip-art style (dark bottle with blank gold label, light-blue wine glass with red wine) — visually inconsistent with the 3D renders; a placeholder reward icon for the wine experiment.
7–10. **Placeholder scoreboard number graphics** (faint light-grey, low-res): **"100%"** (`9171ea47…`, p12,20 = Grade value), **"1"** (`1577b3ab…` = mistakes value), **"15:20"** (`2ad62791…` = time value). These are stand-in art and should become proper TextMeshPro UI.

---

- **p1 — Title card:** "PharmaSynth / Cutscene - After Completing Preparation of Reagents".

---

### Cross-cutting design takeaways for the Unity rebuild
- **Cutscene skeleton (reusable template):** (1) completion modal ("Preparations/Experiment Complete" + confirm button) → (2) robot NPC pops in from a screen corner → (3) camera cuts to robot for a "commend" line → (4) robot gives objective/next-step line while camera pans across the lab to the main table → (5) results scoreboard ("Mission Complete") OR hand-off back to gameplay.
- **NPC:** single recurring robot tutor/mascot; behaviors = corner entrance pop, praise/commend, objective explainer, gesture with floating hands, cyan happy-eye expression. Needs an animation set (enter, idle-float, talk, gesture) and possibly emotive screen-face states.
- **Dialogue system:** subtitle/caption panels only — **no TTS/voice-over**. Plan for a text/subtitle panel UI with timed advance, localizable strings.
- **UI screens to build:** completion modal (title + body + single confirm button), objective/subtitle panel, and a **Mission Complete results scoreboard** with three stats — Grade (%), Number of Mistakes (count), Time (mm:ss) — plus **Continue** and **Retry** actions. Retry implies experiments are replayable and scored.
- **Scoring model implied:** percentage grade, mistake counter, and completion time are tracked per experiment. (Sample values 100% / 1 mistake / 15:20 are placeholders.)
- **Player embodiment:** first-person robotic VR hands; product shown in a petri/watch glass (synthesis experiments) or flask (fermentation).
- **Experiment 8 specifics:** wine-making via fermentation; uses a 1000 mL Erlenmeyer + balloon CO₂ trap; a 7-day/7-night time-skip is dramatized in-cutscene; yields a "wine" byproduct shown as a reward.
- **Production implication:** implement as **in-engine timeline/camera sequences** (e.g., Unity Timeline/Cinemachine) driving a 3D robot + UI overlays — NOT flat pre-rendered video (unsafe in VR). The deck's backgrounds are storyboard stills (some are 3D renders that may double as the actual scene; the 2D wine art and grey number graphics are throwaway placeholders needing real assets).

### Consistency defects to fix in rebuild (present in the source docs)
- The lines "Good job! You have successfully prepared the necessary reagent…" and "Now that you have finished preparing the reagents… proceed in performing the experiment" are **copy-pasted into the post-experiment and wine cutscenes**, where they are contextually wrong. Each cutscene needs context-appropriate dialogue.
- Confirm-button label is inconsistent: **"Okay"** on p8 vs **"Start"** on p16 for the same "Experiment Complete" dialog.
- Scoreboard number values are hard-coded placeholder art (100% / 1 / 15:20) rather than data-driven UI.
key_facts: The PDF is a storyboard/reference deck for THREE cutscenes, not final assets: (1) After Completing Preparation of Reagents (p1-6), (2) After Completing the Experiment (p7-12), (3) Experiment 8 Byproduct - Wine Making (p13-20).

Cutscenes are in-engine scripted camera sequences (explicit camera-pan/cut directions + a 3D NPC that 'pops off the corner of the laboratory'), NOT flat pre-rendered video (which would be uncomfortable in VR). Build with Unity Timeline/Cinemachine.
Dialogue is delivered as on-screen subtitle/caption panels with NO text-to-speech/voice-over (explicit note on p5: 'in a subtitle format (since there's no text-to-speech)').

A single recurring robot mascot NPC (cute white/blue floating chatbot with a screen face showing cyan '^^' happy eyes and detached floating hands) acts as tutor/guide: enters from a corner, commends the player, and explains the next objective.
Player embodiment is first-person VR robotic hands (white segmented plastic fingers, glowing blue wrist rings) on a white lab table.

Reusable cutscene template: completion modal (confirm button) -> robot pops in from corner -> camera cuts to robot 'commend' line -> robot gives objective while camera pans lab to main table -> Mission Complete results scoreboard OR return to gameplay.
Completion modals: 'Preparations Complete' (button 'Start') and 'Experiment Complete' (button 'Okay' on p8, inconsistently 'Start' on p16) with body 'You have successfully completed the experiment!'.

Mission Complete results scoreboard tracks three stats: Grade (percentage), Number of Mistakes (count), Time (mm:ss); sample placeholder values 100% / 1 / 15:20; buttons 'Continue' and 'Retry' (experiments are replayable and scored).

Experiment 8 = wine-making via fermentation: 1000 mL Erlenmeyer flask of dark brown must with an inflated balloon (CO2 capture); a '7 days and 7 nights' time-skip is dramatized; final product shown as wine (bottle + glass).

Only 10 unique image assets exist across the 34 extracted (rest reused): 3D lab (third-person), robot mascot (hi-res + lo-res copies), first-person hands+petri-dish-of-white-powder, first-person hands+wine-flask, isolated wine flask, 2D vector wine bottle+glass, and three faint-grey placeholder number graphics (100%, 1, 15:20).

Visual style is mixed/inconsistent: 3D CGI lab renders + stock stylized 3D robot + FLAT 2D clip-art wine + low-res grey placeholder UI numbers -- the 2D wine art and number graphics are throwaway placeholders needing real assets.

Synthesized products are presented in a glass watch-glass/petri dish as white crystalline powder; the teaching lab is a modern classroom-style lab (blue-grey cabinets, drop-ceiling lights, back bench with balance/analyzer, sink, drying rack, stools).
open_questions: Dialogue copy-paste bug: 'Good job! You have successfully prepared the necessary reagent...' and 'Now that you have finished preparing the reagents... proceed in performing the experiment' are reused verbatim in the post-experiment and wine cutscenes where they are contextually wrong. What is the correct per-cutscene dialogue?

## PharmaSynth Handoff Asset Archive — Read-Only Inventory

Location: `C:/Users/MSI/My project/Docs/handoff_assets/`. Five files. Two are asset archives (the ZIP of raw assets and the Unity project package); two are the same Unity package wrapped/unwrapped; the storyboard/cutscene PDFs are separate design docs (not part of this asset task but noted for gaps).

| File | Size | Nature |
|---|---|---|
| `Assets Capstone-20260706T152116Z-3-001.zip` | 260,767,253 (~249 MB) | Raw/vendor asset bundle: loose icons, 3 GLBs, 8 nested model ZIPs |
| `Transition.unitypackage` | 108,621,400 (~104 MB) | **The previous team's actual Unity project** (scenes, 38 scripts, prefabs, settings) |
| `Transition.zip` | 108,651,859 | **Confirmed pure wrapper**: contains exactly one member `Transition.unitypackage`, uncompressed size 108,621,400 = byte-identical to the standalone. Redundant. |
| `PharmaSynth-Storyboard.pdf` | ~261 MB | Design doc (separate task) |
| `cutscenes-sample.pdf` | ~21 MB | Cutscene sample (separate task) |

---

### ARCHIVE 1 — `Assets Capstone …zip` (raw asset library)

**Top level (loose):**
- **UI Icons (10 PNGs, flat 2D vector):** `Next button.png`, `Back button.png`, `PharmaSynth - VR.png`, `aspirin.png`, `acetone.png`, `Benzamide.png`, `Benzoic Acid.png`, `Choloflorum.png` (chloroform), `Acentalide acid.png` (acetanilide), `Ethyl Synthesis.png`. Art style verified by extracting/viewing: clean **flat minimalist vector medicine/pill-bottle icons** — rounded cap, grey neck, light body, a bold colored label band with a bold sans-serif product name (e.g. red "ASPIRIN", yellow "Chloroform"). These map to experiment products/reagents + menu nav (Next/Back) + app logo. Small (13–64 KB each).
- **Loose GLB models (game-usable, glTF binary):** `RobotNPC.glb` (297 KB — the NPC), `distillation_flask.glb` (1.93 MB — distillation glassware), `shelves_and_drawers.glb` (473 KB — lab storage).

**Nested ZIPs (raw DCC source, NOT auto game-ready — no .meta/prefabs unless noted):**
- **`Laboratory.zip` (100 MB) — the lab environment, Unity-formatted.** Full folder tree with `.meta` files: `Model/Exterior` + `Model/Interior` FBX (walls, floor, ceiling, doors, windows, curtains, vents, lights; AC unit, cabinets, tables×3, wash table, stools, folders, computer/host/keyboard/mouse/screen, printer, hanging exhaust arms, switches, slogans), matching `Materials/`, high-res `Textures/` (Standard-shader naming: `_AlbedoTransparency`, `_MetallicSmoothness`, `_Normal`, `_AO`; several 2K–4K, e.g. `Wall_Normal.png` 7.6 MB, `Computer_baseColor.png` 7.2 MB), `Prefabs/` for every piece, **baked lighting** (`Scenes/Laboratory/` has LightingData.asset, 8 lightmap sets `Lightmap-0..7_comp_light.exr`/`_comp_dir.png`, ReflectionProbe-0.exr), `Settings/*.lighting` + a `LaboratoryProfile.asset` (post-processing/volume). Includes vendor `Documentation.pdf` (1.1 MB) + `Laboratory.json`. NOTE: **no `.unity` scene file** here — only baked lighting for it. Materials are Standard pipeline → **need URP upgrade**.
- **`ChemLabEquipment.zip` (40 MB) — a purchased VR-chem-lab equipment pack, fully game-ready.** Contains: **35+ equipment FBX models** (Beakers 100/500 mL, Erlenmeyer 400 mL, Graduated Cylinder 50 mL, Test tubes + racks (12-tube, drying-pins), Test-tube holders (metal/wooden), Test-tube brush, Dropper, Forceps, Crucible + tongs, Clay triangle, Evaporating dish, Watch glass, Funnel, Glass rod, Mortar+Pestle, Scoopula, Spatula, Iron ring, Retort stand, Tripod, Wire gauze, Vials (+labels), Wash bottles (+labels), **Alcohol burner + Bunsen burner + Balance/scale**); **`Prefabs/`** for all incl. `_WithLiquid` variants; **`Animations/`** (controllers + .anim) for tool actuation: CrucibleTongs open/closed/grasp, Dropper normal/squeezed, Forceps grasp, Test-tube brush bristle, metal & wooden holder grasp; **`Materials/` + `Textures/`** (albedo/metal/normal, burner flame textures, HDRI env `HdriEnvironment.hdr` 5.6 MB); custom **`Shaders/Glass.shader`**; digital **font** `DS-DIGIB.TTF` (for scale/timer readouts); `Scenes/AnimationExample.unity` + `Prefabs.unity` + a **Post-processing Profile**; vendor `Manual.pdf`, `Readme.txt`. **Ships built-in/Standard pipeline PLUS two nested render-pipeline converters: `URP.unitypackage` (380 KB) and `HDRP.unitypackage` (11.9 MB)** — the URP one is the relevant path for this rebuild.
- **`mechanical-pipette(Alternative for Aspirator).zip` (40 MB):** one `Mechanical_Pipette_Full.fbx` (4.4 MB) + full PBR texture set (Base color / Normal-DirectX / Roughness / Metallic / Opacity / Emissive for Pipette, Pusher, Plastic, Metal, Glass parts; several 2–12 MB). Raw model, needs import/material setup.
- **`test-tube-with-cork.zip` (10 MB):** a **Blender `.blend`** (2.4 MB) source + Cork diffuse/normal PNGs (2–5.6 MB). Raw DCC source, not game-ready.
- **`labcoat.zip` (65 MB) — PPE, raw garment source only.** Contains `lab coat and pants_diffuse_1001.png` + nested `source/lab coat and pants.zip` which holds: `lab coat and pants.obj` (19.5 MB mesh) + `.mtl` + diffuse PNG + **Marvelous Designer project `Lab Dress.zprj` (45 MB)** + turntable renders (0/90/180/270.png). **No rig, no Unity prefab** → needs retopo/rig/import to be wearable in VR.
- **`locker.zip` (2 MB):** `locker room.fbx` (688 KB) + PBR textures (BaseColor/Normal/Roughness/Metallic). Raw.
- **`chemical_bottles_nakedsingularity.zip` (1 MB):** `Chemical_bottles_NakedSingularity.fbx` (5 reagent bottles) + JPG/PNG PBR texture sets (glass + plastic variants, T_Chemical_01–05). Raw (Maya-authored, has .mayaSwatches).
- **`thermometer.zip` (163 KB):** `thermometer.glb` + one embedded texture. Game-usable GLB.

### ARCHIVE 2 — `Transition.unitypackage` (previous team's WORKING Unity project — highest-value asset)

378 asset entries; 287 carry a `preview.png` thumbnail. Composition by type: **94 fbx, 58 png, 49 prefab, 44 mat, 38 .cs scripts, 34 jpg, 10 .asset, 9 exr, 1 .unity scene, 1 .inputactions, 1 .md, 1 pdf, 1 json, 1 .wlt, 1 .lighting.** By folder: `Laboratory/` 214 (the env pack re-imported), `Orig Assets/` 92 (raw imported working models), `Scripts/` 42, `Locker/` 8, `Settings/` 8 (URP), `TutorialInfo/` 8, `Prefab/` 2, `Scenes/` 2.

- **Scene:** only ONE — `Assets/Scenes/SampleScene.unity` (the assembled working scene). Plus `Assets/Prefab/Environment.prefab`. Implies a **single-scene, modular** design rather than per-experiment scenes.
- **Render pipeline = URP (confirmed):** `Settings/UniversalRenderPipelineGlobalSettings.asset`, `PC_RPAsset`+`PC_Renderer`, `Mobile_RPAsset`+`Mobile_Renderer` (Quest target), `DefaultVolumeProfile`, `SampleSceneProfile`. `Assets/InputSystem_Actions.inputactions` present (new Input System / XR).
- **`Orig Assets/` (raw working models, 44 FBX):** all ChemLabEquipment items re-imported PLUS distillation-specific extras not in the store pack — **`Reflux Condenser.fbx` (+`_mesh`), `Water_Bath.fbx`, `Thermometer.fbx`/`thermo.fbx`, `RobotNPC.fbx`**, `Chemical_bottles_NakedSingularity.fbx`, `locker room.fbx`, and chemical materials (`chemical1..5_*`, Ethanol/Toluene/Potassium textures). NPC exists as both `.glb` (top-level) and `.fbx` (here).
- **38 C# scripts — the previous team's CODE IS PRESENT.** Grouped:
  - **Experiment framework (`Scripts/Experiment/`):** `ExperimentModuleDefinition.cs` (ScriptableObject: module title, learning outcomes, task checklist — created via menu *Create → VR ChemLab → Experiment Module*), `ExperimentFlowManager.cs` (drives module lifecycle `BeginModule()`, title/status/summary/outcomes TMP UI), `ExperimentTaskTrigger.cs` (collider-based task completion by `taskId`), `TestTaskCompletion.cs`, `ProcedureChecklistUI.cs` (checklist/scoring UI), `ExperimentErrorReporter.cs` (**mistake/error handling**), `FireProcedureZone.cs` (burner-area safety), `WeighingScaleController.cs` (`SetTargetMass/AddMass/RemoveMass`), `EquipmentLabelVisibilityController.cs`, `EthylProcedureActionUI.cs` + `Editor/EthylSynthesisModuleCreator.cs` + `EthylSynthesisDebugTester.cs` (**only the "Ethyl Synthesis"/esterification module is actually built out**), `NPCNarrationController.cs` (**NPC tutorial: AudioSource + subtitle TMP + `SkipNarration()`**), `ModuleCutsceneController.cs` (**cutscenes via `PlayableDirector` sequence + `SkipCutscenes()`**), `TabletGestureController.cs`, `TVRepositionController.cs`, and **`IntegrationGuide.md`** (full wiring doc — see below).
  - **Chemistry/simulation core (`Scripts/`):** `ChemicalData.cs`, `LiquidData.cs`, `LiquidPhysics.cs` (auto-detects/reports wrong-reagent mixing), `LiquidPourer.cs` (curved-stream pour anim), `PowderData?`/`PowderPhysics.cs`, `PowderPourer.cs`, `ReactionRule.cs` + `ReactionRegistry.cs` (**reaction rule system**), `Testsol.cs`, `TimerController.cs`, `XRBottleUI.cs`, `Wobble.cs`, `UI/MoveInstructionsOnTilt.cs`.
  - **`Scripts/misc/`:** `AcidCorrosion.cs`, `SaltBurn.cs`, `SpoonSaltController.cs`, `ChemLabelUpdater.cs`, `Tunneling.cs` (VR comfort vignette), `UIfuncs.cs`.
  - **`TutorialInfo/`:** Unity starter Readme system (`Readme.cs`, `ReadmeEditor.cs`, `Readme.asset`, `Layout.wlt`) — boilerplate, low value.
- **`IntegrationGuide.md` (full text captured)** documents 8 wiring sections: (1) Module + Score UI via `ExperimentModuleDefinition` + `ExperimentFlowManager` (assign moduleTitle/status/summary/outcomes TMP, outcomes/summary panels, call `BeginModule()` from Start button); (2) Task completion + mistake reporting (`ExperimentTaskTrigger.taskId`, `ExperimentErrorReporter`, auto wrong-reagent via `LiquidPhysics`, `FireProcedureZone` around burners); (3) NPC tutorial (AudioSource + subtitle + skip); (4) Cutscenes (`PlayableDirector` sequence + skip); (5) Weighing scale events; (6) Glass break/safety (`BreakableGlassware`: broken-glass prefab + particle + shatter audio + floor tag + impact threshold); (7) Improved pour (streamSegments/dropStrength/flowSmoothing/min-maxStreamWidth); (8) Labels/tablet-gesture/TV-reposition.

---

### GAP ASSESSMENT vs required build

| Need | Status in archives |
|---|---|
| Lab environment | ✅ Strong — full `Laboratory` pack (models, prefabs, baked lighting) + `shelves_and_drawers.glb` + locker room. Standard-shader materials → URP upgrade required. Scene assembled in `SampleScene.unity`. |
| Glassware / general equipment | ✅ Excellent — `ChemLabEquipment` pack (beakers, flasks, cylinders, test tubes/racks/holders, droppers, tongs, forceps, mortar/pestle, burners, balance, wash bottles, vials) with prefabs, animations, `_WithLiquid` variants; URP converter included. Plus mechanical pipette, distillation flask, thermometer, chemical bottles. |
| Distillation | ✅ Glassware present: `distillation_flask.glb`, `Reflux Condenser.fbx`, `Water_Bath.fbx`, thermometer, retort/iron-ring/stand. |
| Fermentation / wine-making | ⚠️ No dedicated fermentation vessel/carboy/wine assets identified — must be built or substituted from beakers/flasks. |
| Oxidation / benzoic acid / benzamide / acetone / chloroform / acetanilide | ⚠️ **Only UI icons exist** for these products; no dedicated per-experiment apparatus beyond the generic glassware; only the **Ethyl Synthesis (esterification)** experiment is actually coded. |
| Acetylation / aspirin / esterification | ⚠️ Icons + generic glassware; esterification ("Ethyl Synthesis") is the one built-out module (has creator/UI/debug scripts). |
| NPC robot | ✅ `RobotNPC.glb` + `RobotNPC.fbx` + `NPCNarrationController.cs`. ⚠️ No rig/animation/voice clips confirmed. |
| PPE / lab coat | ⚠️ Raw only — OBJ + Marvelous Designer `.zprj`, unrigged, not VR-wearable as-is. |
| UI icons | ✅ 10 flat-vector icons (products + Next/Back + logo). More may be needed per experiment/step. |
| Cutscene media | ❌ **None in asset archives** — `ModuleCutsceneController` expects `PlayableDirector`/Timeline assets, but there are **zero Timeline/Playable/video files**. Storyboard/cutscene content lives only in the separate PDFs. |
| Animations | ⚠️ Only tool actuation anims inside `ChemLabEquipment.zip`; the working unitypackage contains **no .anim/.controller/Timeline** at all. |
| Audio / SFX / voice | ❌ **NONE anywhere** — verified no .wav/.mp3/.ogg/.mp4 in the unitypackage or any nested ZIP, despite scripts referencing AudioSource (NPC narration, shatter audio). All voice/SFX must be produced. |

**Pipeline caveats for rebuild:** Laboratory + ChemLabEquipment base materials are built-in/Standard shader → run URP upgrade (URP converter is bundled). Custom `Glass.shader` and post-processing profiles may need URP porting. Marvelous Designer lab coat and .blend test-tube need DCC processing. `Transition.zip` is a redundant duplicate of the unitypackage — ignore it.
